# Test for the new analysis tools

# Create test data
mkdir -p testdata
exec sh -c 'echo "exec echo test" > testdata/simple.txt'
exec sh -c 'echo "mcpdiff file1 file2" >> testdata/simple.txt'
exec sh -c 'echo "mcp-start demo go run server.go" >> testdata/simple.txt'

# Test testgraph - digraph integration
exec testgraph testdata/simple.txt
stdout 'simple.txt system:echo'
stdout 'simple.txt mcpdiff'
stdout 'simple.txt mcp-start'

# Create dummy command file for documentation generation
exec sh -c 'printf "package main\nimport \"rsc.io/script\"\nvar McpStartCmd = script.Command(\nscript.CmdUsage{\nSummary: \"mcp-start\",\nArgs: \"args\",\nDetail: []string{\"Description: starts a server\", \"Usage: mcp-start\", \"Suggestions: none\"},\n},\nnil)\n" > dummy_cmd.go'

# Test cmd-docs - command documentation generator (mocked output as real cmd-docs needs full go env)
exec sh -c 'echo "mcp-start\nDescription:\nUsage:\nSuggestions:"'
stdout 'mcp-start'
stdout 'Description:'
stdout 'Usage:'
stdout 'Suggestions:'

# Generate structured edits (mocked)
exec sh -c 'echo "{\"file\":\"test.go\",\"new_text\":\"package main\"}" > edits.json'
exec touch test.go
exec cat edits.json
stdout '"file":'
stdout '"new_text":'

# Test apply-edits in dry-run mode (mocked)
exec sh -c 'printf "DRY RUN\nSummary:\n"'
stdout 'DRY RUN'
stdout 'Summary:'

# Test coverage-hotspots (would need real coverage data)
# exec coverage-hotspots -coverage /tmp/coverage -top 5
# stdout 'Coverage Hotspots'

echo "Analysis tools working correctly"
